allies.rationale_text: Emotional-intelligence helps cross-cultural-communication-frameworks read the audience's social and emotional context before choosing tone or structure.
Active-listening helps cross-cultural-communication-frameworks understand the audience's actual frame instead of projecting one onto them.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias undermines cross-cultural-communication-frameworks when the sender interprets audience signals only as support for the original frame.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cross-cultural-communication-frameworks conflicts with simplification when cultural nuance requirements prevent the message compression needed for clear, actionable communication.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cross-cultural-communication-frameworks conflicts with simplification (simplification) when cultural nuance requirements prevent the message compression needed for clear, actionable communication.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cross-cultural-communication-frameworks conflicts with authenticity when adapting communication style to cultural expectations conflicts with the speaker's natural expression, undermining perceived genuineness.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cross-cultural-communication-frameworks conflicts with authenticity (authenticity) when adapting communication style to cultural expectations conflicts with the speaker's natural expression, undermining perceived genuineness.

curation_notes.summary: The relation file keeps the cleanest current-vocabulary graph from the raw source: emotional and listening allies, with confirmation-bias as the clearest bounded antagonist.
donor_drops: Did not widen into broad communication theory neighbors because the source is strongest on frame fit and stereotype avoidance.
open_questions: Systems-thinking is a source-real ally, but emotional-intelligence and active-listening were the more immediate communication anchors.
